name: ai-privacy-notice
title: AI Privacy Notice
description: Compact inline disclosure of how prompt data is used, retained, and processed externally.

props.name: facts
props.description: Disclosure lines: { id, kind?, label, detail? }. Every fact is caller-supplied — the component asserts nothing about your data handling on its own.

props.name: facts[].kind
props.description: Drives the leading icon so the four disclosures are scannable.

props.name: facts[].detail
props.description: The specifics behind the label — processor, region, legal basis, deletion route. Shown on hover/focus and always exposed to assistive technology.

props.name: detailHref
props.description: Link to the product's authoritative privacy notice or controls.

clinicalNotes: The hazard this guards against is a clinician pasting identifiable detail into a prompt without knowing where that text is about to go. The disclosure therefore sits at the point of the action rather than behind a settings page, and it names the four things a clinician actually needs before pressing send: what leaves the device, whether identifiers are stripped, who processes it and where, and how long it is kept. Because those answers differ by deployment, the component hard-codes none of them — a notice that ships with confident defaults would be a false one. The `detail` text is not hidden information: it is rendered into the accessible name of each line as well as into the tooltip, so a screen-reader or keyboard user is never asked to consent to a hover. This is a disclosure, not a control and not a consent record: it does not gate the send action, it does not capture agreement, and it never replaces the product's privacy notice or its opt-outs. Use ai-consent-notice where agreement must actually be collected.
notes: Composes alert + tooltip. Designed to sit directly above or beside ai-prompt-input's send action; keep it to three or four facts so it stays readable at a glance.
